commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Evening Whale Quest with Alaskan Cuisine
commerceProducts.description: Embark on an evening cruise where you'll enjoy Alaskan inspired appetizers and with guaranteed whale sightings. Get the most of your day in Juneau, before boarding one of Alaska's best passenger boats. Relax and unwind in the warm, spacious main cabin as you journey into Stephens Passage, a breathtaking marine wilderness surrounded by snow-capped peaks and glaciers. This area is the favored summer feeding ground for humpback whales. Marvel as the whales arc their massive tail flukes into the air. Your captain will navigate the boat close enough to allow exceptional viewing. Learn about these magnificent mammals from your onboard naturalist and discover the other incredible species that call this wilderness home including orcas, sea lions, porpoises, harbor seals, bald eagles and more. The culinary experience highlights locally-sourced cuisine. The menu includes Alaska themed appetizers, seasonal vegetables, meat and cheese tray and various delicious desserts. Complimentary tea, coffee, and cocoa will be available. Locally brewed beer, wine and sodas are available for purchase.
highlights: Listen to an expert onboard naturalist explanation the behavior and habitat of the humpback whale and other wildlife.
Try whale sightings from your catamaran's warm, spacious main cabin as you set out to explore Stephen's Passage.
Throughout the cruise indulge in locally sourced Alaskan appetizers.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Musher's Camp and Gold Panning
commerceProducts.description: Your journey begins with an entertaining historic tour from your knowledgeable guide through downtown Skagway and along nine miles of stunning coastline to our Adventure Camp in Dyea. Kick off your visit with what we like to call a “Musher Talk.” We have been sharing our passion for dog sledding for years, and in that time, we have gathered up the most frequently asked questions about the sport. In the Musher Talk, we will share the fascinating answers and welcome any additional questions you may have. Of course, we make sure you have plenty of time to meet some of the actual athletes who do these amazing things, our sled dogs! After that, have a chance to pan for gold in our riverside sluice! Once your guide shows you the art of gold panning, you can test your luck to see what you can find. Then you will have time to browse the gift shop and relax in our saloon with a deck overlooking the picturesque stream.
highlights: Enjoy a scenic coach journey through downtown Skagway, viewing nine miles of breathtaking Alaska coastline.
Learn about Alaskan dog mushing and meet the sled dogs.
Your guide will show you the art of gold panning, test your luck to see what you can find.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Mendenhall Glacier, Whale Watching and Wildlife Quest
commerceProducts.description: Experience Alaska’s two most popular attractions – glaciers and wildlife – on one incredible excursion. Begin with a panoramic drive through the Mendenhall Valley to Auke Bay, where you’ll board an Alaskan made expedition vessel for a whale-watching cruise. Relax in the spacious heated cabin as your Captain navigates through the island-studded waters of Stephen’s Passage. You’re most likely to spend time viewing the world famous Humpback Whales and sleek Killer Whales. Also look for porpoises, Bald Eagles, Harbor Seals, Sea Lions, and other wildlife. Afterwards, enjoy a narrated drive to Mendenhall Glacier. Trek along scenic viewing trails for incredible photo opportunities, and learn about the awesome force of glaciers from Forest Service experts at the Mendenhall Visitor Center.
highlights: Explore Alaska’s spectacular wilderness by land and sea on a combination motor coach and catamaran adventure.
Discover the stunning blue-ice Mendenhall Glacier and take in the beauty of Juneau’s unspoiled landscape while traveling through Mendenhall Valley.
Glide along the pristine waters of Stephen’s Passage and enjoy incredible views of snow-capped mountains, towering glaciers, and Alaska’s abundant wildlife.

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require leisurely walking over primarily even surfaces. There may be a limited number of steps, a minimal amount of cobblestones or uneven surfaces, and some standing for extended periods of time.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Experience Victoria by E-Bicycle
commerceProducts.description:
A two-hour sightseeing electric-assisted bicycle ride through Victoria's remarkable streetscapes offers a unique view of the city, as you pedal through Beacon Hill Park along Dallas Road Waterfront Trail through Chinatown and into the Inner Harbour. Meet up with your team of professional rider guides for a quick safety briefing. Then hop on two wheels and ride behind a guide who will lead you along an extensive network of paths and urban bike lanes throughout this "Cycling Capital of Canada". Your sightseeing route lets you explore the south end of Vancouver Island, where you'll admire Victorian architecture and a vibrant city vibe infused with a British colonial past. Breathe in the aroma of colorful flowers while traveling woodland trails through Beacon Hill Park. Feel the salty air rush through your hair along the Dallas Road Waterfront Trail, a recreational area popular with sailboarders and dog walkers. Pass under the ornate Gate of Harmonious Interest, which welcomes you to the oldest Chinatown in Canada. Then direct your e-bike toward the Inner Harbour, a seaside enclave abuzz with boats and float planes. Here you can admire First Nation totem poles and take in the beautiful British Columbia landscape before pedaling back to the pier.
This tour is provided by an operator that has been third-party certified to meet the Global Sustainable Tourism Council's Criteria for Tour Operators.
highlights: Embark on a two-wheel, electrically assisted sightseeing adventure through Victoria with an experienced guide who knows the city's best routes.
With more cyclists per capita than any other Canadian city, Victoria offers epic bicycling opportunities.
Pedal past pretty Victorian architecture, Beacon Hill Park, Dallas Road Waterfront Trail, Chinatown's colorful cityscapes, and the Inner Harbour.

activityLevel.description: This tour is not recommended for guests with pre-existing medical conditions such as cardiac, respiratory, or orthopedic conditions, including joint injuries or disease, or any other serious medical condition. commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Whale Watching and Wildlife Quest
commerceProducts.description: Embark on a spectacular sightseeing cruise to discover some of Alaska's most majestic creatures - humpback whales. Your quest begins aboard a waterjet-powered expedition vessel specially designed for wildlife viewing. Relax in the warm, comfortable, spacious main cabin, surrounded by large windows as your experienced captain guides you through the island-studded waters of Stephen's Passage. Against a majestic backdrop of snow-capped peaks and glaciers, the onboard naturalist will explain the behavior and habitat of wildlife you may encounter, including humpback and killer whales, Steller sea lions, Dall's porpoise, harbor seals, bald eagles and more.  To fully enjoy the sights and sounds of a close encounter with wildlife, be sure to bring a warm, rainproof jacket to wear on the topside observation (open-air) deck and don't forget your camera! Complimentary snacks and beverages are provided.
highlights: Sail across Stephen's Passage aboard an expedition vessel built in the tour operators very own shipyard.
Gaze upon dozens of Alaska's most elusive wildlife.
Catch spectacular views of snow-capped mountains and glaciers.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: To The Summit!
commerceProducts.description: Adventure to the 3,292 foot Summit of White Pass by mini-bus in just 1.5 hours Step off at scenic viewpoints for breathtaking photos! Your mountain guide will pass on a century of stories. You will see Dead Horse Trail, the White Pass Railroad, Pitchfork Falls, Moore bridge and numerous glaciers and peaks during your personalized, group experience. Stop at the Summit and and enjoy magnificent views of the scenic mountain landscape before returning to Skagway through USCBP. Upon returning to Skagway , a stop is made in town for guests wishing to shop or explore independently.
highlights: Discover Alaska's spectacular White Pass Summit, via the Klondike Highway, known as the original Gold Rush Trail of' '98.
Small group experience with local guide,
See Skagway and White Pass in just 1.5 hour, combines well with other tours

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Klondike Summit, Gold Panning and Prospectors Lunch
commerceProducts.description: Discover Skagway's rich gold-mining history as you traverse the scenic Klondike Highway aboard a comfortable coach, viewing historic landmarks and vestiges of Gold Rush days along the way. Then meet vivacious pioneer characters, where you'll also try your hand at gold panning. Linger over an open-air rustic feast as you approach a tasty spread of Alderwood-grilled WILD Alaska salmon, Cheechako chicken, Chilkoot baked beans, Tongass wild-rice pilaf, a selection of sides, salads, and beverages. Eat some blueberry cake for dessert as a sweet finish.
highlights: Gaze in awe at majesty of the Alaskan wilderness as you journey across the Klondike Highway.
Follow in the footsteps of old-time gold seekers as you pan for the precious metal.
Learn about Skagway's role in the Gold Rush, an ear when many risked everything in hopes of riches.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Adventure Hike Skagway
commerceProducts.description: Join experienced guides on an exciting hiking adventure through the Tongass Rainforest that immerses you in the incredible beauty of Southeast Alaska. Your adventure will begin with a guided driving town tour of Skagway and continue up the White Pass on the Klondike Highway. Along the way your guide will regale you with history about the Klondike Gold Rush and the natural history of the area. From the trailhead you will spend the next two hours hiking through the beautiful alpine forest surrounded by the towering snow-capped mountains of the White Pass. Your naturalist guides will stop frequently along the way to share their knowledge about the native flora, fauna, and to describe the amazing glacial history of Alaska. Each group is outfitted with binoculars to maximize your opportunities to see up close the spectacular mountain and river scenery. Midway through the hike you will be treated to a delicious snack at the final breathtaking overlook with incredible views of the Skagway valley, White Pass, and the Lynn Canal.
highlights: Drive through the town of Skagway en route to Alaska's famous White Pass, where your trailhead begins.
Spend approximately 2 hours hiking through the alpine rainforest, surrounded by the snowcapped mountains of the White Pass.
Experience the Tongass National Forest, the nation’s largest rainforest.
Learn about the native flora, fauna, and the glacial history of Alaska
Breathtaking overlooks with beautiful views

commerceProducts.description: Experience Skagway's gold rush history on this adventure to the infamous White Pass Summit. Enjoy a narrated scenic drive in the comfort of a motor coach past the wooden boardwalks and façades of downtown Skagway. Then, follow in the footsteps of early gold seekers as you journey 3292 feet above sea level to White Pass Summit. You'll see Brackett Wagon Road, the breathtaking Pitchfork Falls, and Tormented Valley. Admire incredible views while learning about a significant moment in Alaskan history.

Enjoy a guided drive through downtown Skagway, hearing stories of Skagway settlers during the Gold Rush.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Whale Watching and Wildlife Tour
commerceProducts.description: Experience a remarkably comfortable and captivating whale and wildlife cruise aboard Victoria’s premier whale watching vessels. This high-speed sightseeing vessel affords all sorts of added conveniences. Look out from the open deck or relax in the headed indoor seating area. Enjoy a snack onboard; perhaps even browse a wildlife book from the onboard collection. Keep watch for the telltale spouts of majestic Humpback Whales. Victoria’s waters are also the best place in the world to spot mighty Killer Whales. A team of  certified marine biologists travels on every voyage to provide in-depth details about marine mammals and other wildlife found along the way. Cruise through this remarkably pristine territory and have your camera at the ready, as you set sail with Victoria’s largest whale and wildlife spotting fleet.
highlights: Relax aboard the luxurious sightseeing vessel and enjoy a snack during your voyage. Cruise the waters off Victoria in search of Killer Whales, Humpback Whales, porpoises, seals and sea lions, along with other marine wildlife. Learn about whales and the marine ecosystem from a team of marine biologists.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Ketchikan Pub Crawl
commerceProducts.description: Let's raise a glass to history! This fun, informative and engaging 90-minute walking tour is sure to please anyone who wants to really get to know the local culture of Ketchikan. You'll embark on your pub crawl with a true Alaskan local who will share the colorful history of the bar scene through stories and vintage photographs. You'll hear infamous stories of days gone by, facts of the prohibition era when the red-light district was in full swing and the legacy of many of Ketchikan’s most popular hot spots. You’ll enjoy an adult beverage at each stop, stroll down historic streets and get a real sense of what life is like in this unique rainforest town. Join us and belly up to the bar in Ketchikan!
highlights: Meet your local guide and set out to explore some of Ketchikan's favorite hot spots.
Hear stories of the Prohibition era and Ketchikan's Red Light District .

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Klondike Bicycle Tour
commerceProducts.description: Feel the rush of riding a bike down the historic Klondike Highway on this thrilling cycling adventure. Your journey begins with a lovely sightseeing drive to the summit of White Pass. Follow the footsteps of fortune-seeking stampeders who travelled this road more than a century ago. Learn about the thousands of gold-seeking stampeders who followed this trail in 1898. Listen to tales of the Klondike’s past and present from your professional guide. Then, feel the rush of riding your bike 15 miles down the historic Klondike Highway. Breathe in the fresh mountain air as you coast down this scenic mountain road. Learn about the ecology of the area from your knowledgeable expert guides. Admire the breathtaking mountains, glaciers, and the historic Yukon Railroad.
highlights: Retrace the historic Gold Rush route while coasting down from the White Pass summit on a bicycle.
Enjoy a narrated sightseeing drive through historic Skagway and up the coastal mountains to the summit.
Experience historic White Pass and Yukon Route Railroad, cascading waterfalls, awe-inspiring glaciers, majestic mountains, and historic sites.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Experience Victoria
commerceProducts.description:
Head out on a one-hour sightseeing coach drive that includes a quick glimpse of Victoria's city highlights and Inner Harbour, plus enjoy optional free time to shop and dine around downtown as you wish. Listen along to an informative guide as you travel in a comfortable bus around the capital of British Columbia. Trace the city's colonial past while viewing its Victorian architecture, Royal BC Museum, and Parliament Buildings. You'll pass through the second-oldest Chinatown in North America and see Mile O of the Trans Canada Highway. Admire the greenery of Beacon Hill Park and the grandiose stature of the luxurious Fairmont Empress Hotel. Along the Inner Harbour waterfront you'll explore the city's First Nations heritage and shipbuilding and commercial fishing industries that thrive at the southern end of Vancouver Island. In the heart of downtown you can either return by bus, traipsing through James Bay, the city's oldest and most charming neighborhoods. Or opt to stay awhile longer, wandering into local shops and eating British Columbian food before making your own way back to the ship.

This tour is provided by an operator that has been third-party certified to meet the Global Sustainable Tourism Council's Criteria for Tour Operators.
highlights: Victoria: Enjoy a commentated sightseeing drive past the Parliament Buildings, Royal BC Museum, Fairmont Empress Hotel and Beacon Hill Park.
Inner Harbour: Embrace the city's seafaring spirit while learning about its First Nations roots, shipbuilding industry and commercial fishing operations.
Downtown: Ride through the city's oldest neighborhoods or opt to explore shops and restaurants before returning to the ship independently.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Talbot Lake Rainforest Hike
commerceProducts.description: Explore the last frontier on an amazing trek through the Tongass National Forest, one of the last intact temperate rainforests in the world, during a hike that invites you to immerse yourself in the immense, unique beauty of Alaska.
highlights: A short drive delivers you into the heart of the Alaskan wilderness and a leisurely hike among the giant coniferous trees of the Tongass National Forest, the largest national forest in the United States.

Walk over the moss-covered grounds of the old-growth forest and listen to the trickle of flowing streams, punctuated by the calls of dozens of birds chirping above as your guide narrates the story of this illustrious natural habitat.
Arrive at the scenic Talbot Lake, where a warm campfire set against the natural backdrop of the surrounding forest awaits.
Recharge with a snack during a quick break, tighten those laces for the return hike to the trail entrance before boarding your shuttle for the return drive to the pier.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Victoria Highlights and History Walking Tour
commerceProducts.description: Walk with a knowledgeable local guide to learn about Victoria's scenic, cultural, architectural, and historical highlights on a tour that includes city attractions that can only be reached on foot.
highlights: Meet your guide at the pier and set out to discover Victoria on foot, a tour that’s sure to fascinate and entertain. Along the way, stroll along the scenic oceanfront through historic James Bay, the oldest residential neighborhood in the city. From here, continue to the breathtaking downtown Inner Harbor, a bustling commercial core with regular seaplane departures to the surrounding islands, street performers, local crafts, and food vendors.

The Inner Harbor is also home to the Royal BC Museum, BC Legislative Buildings, and iconic Fairmont Empress Hotel. Walk to Victoria's stunning,19th-century Parliament Buildings, home to British Columbian government since 1897, and get up close in the walk-only areas that take you to the front entrance of these neoclassical gem. Arrive at Thunderbird Park to see its towering totem poles and ornate aboriginal art before continuing to the University of Victoria campus and stopping at the Aboriginal Long House, built in the traditional Coast Salish style. During your visit, your guide shares tall stories, the history of the area, and an insider’s knowledge of the city of Victoria. Following your tour, you may continue with your group for the guided walk back to the pier via the James Bay neighborhood or opt to remain in the downtown area for shopping and exploration, and return to the pier at your leisure.

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require extended periods of walking over even and uneven surfaces, steep terrain and/or water activity in a slight current. commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Ghost and Goodtime Girls Walking Tour
commerceProducts.description:
Join a madam from the Red Onion Saloon for a fun and informative walking tour through downtown Skagway!
First, you’ll be greeted at the pier by a lavishly costumed and highly entertaining Madam. After a quick shuttle transfer to town, she will guide you through the back alleys and former red-light districts of Skagway. Here, women of the evening brushed shoulders with notorious characters, wide-eyed stampeders, and dangerous odds. Stories of Klondike Kate, Cad Wilson and Diamond Lil depict the adventurous stories of the women who stampeded north. You will pass historic and haunted gold rush buildings as you learn of Skagway’s rich history and the women who helped make it what it is.
Your experience culminates with a stop at the world-famous Red Onion Saloon’s restored Brothel Museum. Here, join in a champagne toast and explore the hallways and bedrooms of this celebrated brothel, observing original relics and images of the ladies who worked there. Bring your camera and you may even catch a glimpse of the resident ghost, Lydia!
*Your tour concludes at the Red Onion Saloon and guests may choose to stay in town and explore more of Skagway or take the short walk back to the pier.
highlights: Visit the Red Onion Saloon and Brothel Museum and see the artifacts left behind by the working women over 125 years ago.
Explore Skagway's back streets and be entertained by your masterful guide and Madam on this walking tour that's been featured on podcasts, ghost hunting shows, and even the Discovery channel.

commerceProducts.title: Picture Perfect Victoria
commerceProducts.description: Discover Victoria’s stunning landscapes and vibrant charm on this photographic walking shore excursion that leaves you with both enhanced skills and a gallery of stunning images to treasure.
highlights: Meet your guide at port for a leisurely walk through the historic James Bay neighborhood – one of Victoria’s oldest residential areas – featuring charming, heritage-style homes and streets lined with colorful gardens.
Your guide points out ideal vantage points and offers practical tips for framing shots and using natural light to enhance your images.
Continue your journey along the picturesque Dallas Road, a popular oceanside pathway offering expansive views of the sparkling water and rugged coastline.
Stop at the Mile "0" marker, the symbolic start of the Trans-Canada Highway, for an iconic photo opportunity.

After, arrive at the sprawling Beacon Hill Park, a 200-acre oasis filled with photography inspiration. Your guide leads you to the park’s highlights, such as free-roaming peacocks, manicured gardens, and serene ponds.
Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced photographer, your guide offers hands-on advice for perfecting your technique, from adjusting camera settings to creating engaging compositions.
As your Picture Perfect Victoria excursion concludes, enjoy a relaxed walk back to port with your guide, enjoying any final photo opportunities along the way.

commerceProducts.title: Great Fermentations! Wine and Distillery Tour
commerceProducts.description: Undertake a journey of discovery that seamlessly blends the region’s finest wines, spirits, and scenery with its rich history and charm, making it an ideal shore excursion for those seeking to indulge in the best of British Columbia.
highlights: Begin your journey with a scenic drive through Victoria’s idyllic countryside, where rolling vineyards and lush landscapes provide a serene backdrop.
Arrive at a charming local estate winery, renowned for its award-winning vintages and stunning grounds.
After a guided tour, settle into a picturesque tasting room, where you sample a selection of wines, from crisp whites to bold reds, thoughtfully paired with locally sourced cheeses.
Next, travel to a local brewery and distillery, where the focus shifts to craft beer and spirits. Enjoy a tasting of two artisan whiskies, crafted with care and aged to perfection.

After indulging in the countryside’s finest beverages, head back to downtown Victoria for a guided sightseeing tour. Stroll through the charming streets of Old Town, pass by the stately Legislative Buildings, and catch stunning views of the Inner Harbour.
No tour of Victoria would be complete without a visit to the iconic Empress Hotel, a historic landmark known for its grandeur and timeless elegance.
Conclude your Great Fermentations! Wine and Distillery excursion by returning to port, filled with memories of Victoria’s scenic beauty, flavorful discoveries, and rich cultural heritage."

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Kitchen Science Alaska Garden to Table Food Tour
commerceProducts.description: Experience a piece of American history – and cuisine - with a visit to the historic Skagway Inn, where you mingle with local Alaskans, immerse yourself in centuries of tradition and discover the culinary secrets of this charming rural community.
highlights: After a brief tour through the city of Skagway, meet with the chef and owner of the historic Skagway Inn and embark on a behind-the-scenes tour of the facilities, as you discover the science that transforms garden-grown ingredients into delicious meals.

Stroll through the inn’s culinary garden and learn about the foods and herbs native to Alaska, along with the techniques used to catch and smoke fresh local fish. Then, head inside the inn, which once served as a brothel during the Klondike Gold Rush.

Here, learn the science behind making a loaf of sourdough bread, as you feed the establishment’s sourdough starter and savor some organic veggies, before transforming simple ingredients from the garden into a sweet concoction – your very own homemade ice cream.

Finish your culinary journey with a hint of luxury, as you enjoy a golden, non-alcoholic drink - a tasty reminder of the town's gold rush past - before returning to your awaiting ship with a few new recipes and skills to bring back home.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Historic Ketchikan Trolley
commerceProducts.description: Step aboard a festive trolley and be entertained by your guide’s engaging stories about Ketchikan, renowned as “Alaska’s First City” and the “Salmon Capital of the World.” As you traverse narrow downtown streets, you'll pass by colorful landmarks, bustling boat harbors and the scenic Ketchikan Creek. Your driver guide will share stories that provide a fascinating glimpse into the city's past and present, with special attention to its historical significance and lively atmosphere. Your first stop is the Alaska Rainforest Sanctuary. Discover the beauty of Alaska’s temperate rainforest on this 40-acre private reserve, offering an up-close look at the landscape, wildlife, and culture that make this region so special. Begin with a private stop at Herring Cove, where you might spot harbor seals, bald eagles, and even black bears from a scenic viewing platform. Wrap up your visit with time at the General Store to browse for souvenirs and enjoy a drink at the cozy coffee shop or bar. This relaxed and enriching tour blends natural wonders, wildlife, and Native heritage for a truly memorable experience. Once reboarding the trolley, you have the option of returning to the ship or being dropped off in downtown Ketchikan to explore on your own and return to the ship at your leisure.
highlights: Step aboard a historic trolley and enjoy the colorful sights of Alaska's first city.
View a collection of original Native totem poles highlighting Alaska's indigenous craftsmanship.
Discover how Ketchikan's Native Alaskan heritage and industrial development shaped its history and evolution to the present day.
